CREATE TABLE timestamps_test (id INT PRIMARY KEY, event_time TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO timestamps_test VALUES (1, localtimestamp()), (2, localtimestamp()), (3, localtimestamp());
SELECT id, event_time FROM timestamps_test WHERE event_time = localtimestamp();
SELECT id FROM timestamps_test WHERE event_time < localtimestamp() AND id IN (SELECT id FROM timestamps_test WHERE id > 1);
CREATE TABLE events (event_id INT PRIMARY KEY, event_time TIMESTAMP, description TEXT);
INSERT INTO events VALUES (1, localtimestamp(), 'Event 1'), (2, localtimestamp(), 'Event 2');
SELECT e.event_id, e.description FROM events e JOIN timestamps_test t ON e.event_time = t.event_time WHERE localtimestamp() = t.event_time;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM timestamps_test WHERE event_time >= localtimestamp() - INTERVAL '1 hour';
SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events WHERE event_time > localtimestamp() - INTERVAL '24 hours') AS recent_events_count;
SELECT e.event_id, e.description FROM events e WHERE e.event_time IN (SELECT localtimestamp() FROM dual);
SELECT t1.id, t1.event_time FROM timestamps_test t1 JOIN timestamps_test t2 ON t1.event_time > t2.event_time WHERE t2.event_time < localtimestamp();
